gazette: Official Gazette dated February 28, 2024, and numbered 32474
title: Determination of Cases in Which Payment Obligations Arising from Contracts and Sales Agreed in Foreign Currency or Indexed to Foreign Currency Can Be Made in Foreign Currency
bodyHtml:
On February 28, 2024, the "Communiqué Amending the Communiqué on the Decree No. 32 on the Protection of the Value of Turkish Currency (Communiqué No: 2008-32/34)" was published in the Official Gazette numbered 32474.

With the amendment, it has been allowed for payment obligations arising from "contracts and sales agreed in foreign currency or indexed to foreign currency" made among residents in Turkey to be made in foreign currency in the following cases:

  • Payment obligations within the scope of securities sales contracts for exports to be conducted through Foreign Trade Capital Companies or Sectoral Foreign Trade Companies and companies with the status of Export Consortium and E-Export Consortium,
  • Payment obligations within the scope of sale and delivery of goods, transfer of goods in transit, transfer of goods in customs warehouses and temporary storage places, and payment obligations for the sale of goods in free zones,
  • Payment obligations for the delivery of goods subject to securities sales contracts conducted by companies operating in free zones and for goods subject to securities sales contracts within the scope of foreign trade transactions.

These payment obligations can be fulfilled in foreign currency as of February 28, 2024.
